title: Hash Tables & Collisions
description: Master hash tables, hash functions, collision resolution mechanisms, and O(1) data lookup.
learningObjectives: Explain key-to-index mapping via hash functions
Resolve collisions using Chaining or Open Addressing
Identify performance costs of hashing lookups in real-time updates

sections.title: Hash Map Internals and Collisions
sections.content:
Hash tables map key objects to indices in a flat array using a Hash Function, enabling O(1) lookups. When two unique keys produce the same index (a Collision), the table resolves it via:

1. **Chaining (Buckets)**: Storing colliding nodes in a linked list at that index.
2. **Open Addressing**: Finding another empty slot nearby (Linear Probing, Double Hashing).

sections.title: Simple Hash Function
